focus_points.label: Shipping confidence and reassurance timing.
focus_points.reason: Week three signal: he hesitates before shipping and asks for reassurance late. Could be a normal confidence dip, or a tooling/expectation gap we can fix quickly—worth clarifying what specifically makes shipping feel risky right now.

focus_points.label: What 'done' means in his onboarding handoffs.
focus_points.reason: Onboarding handoffs still feel fuzzy in places. One possibility is he’s compensating by over-checking because the success bar and acceptance steps aren’t crisp yet—map the exact handoff points that are unclear so you can tighten them for the next two weeks.

focus_points.label: Right support touchpoints for the next two weeks.
focus_points.reason: Whether he’s a junior or not, onboarding succeeds when the manager and team make support predictable: who to ask, when to review, and what kind of reassurance is available before work reaches the “late” stage.

focus_points.label: Tooling or process friction slowing his loop.
focus_points.reason: Junior engineers often stall when the daily loop isn’t smooth—CI/review tooling, unclear steps, or missing context. Worth identifying the specific friction points so you can remove them quickly rather than treating it as confidence alone.
